summary: Simon Späti recounts his journey from traditional editors like Notepad++ and VS Code to adopting Vim and Markdown as the foundation of his entire workflow. He describes how learning Vim's modal editing philosophy transformed his efficiency, comparing it to surgical precision with text. The transition from WordPress to static site generation with Markdown files eliminated formatting battles and gave him a single source of truth. Combined with Obsidian for note-taking (with Vim keybindings enabled), he now writes everything from notes to a full book in plain text, publishing with simple scripts.
key_points: Editor shortcuts and productivity gains were lost whenever switching tools, but Vim motions work everywhere
Vim's modal editing philosophy (normal, insert, visual, command modes) enables surgical precision with text that traditional editors cannot match
Moving from WordPress to Markdown static sites eliminated the hassle of maintaining two versions and formatting battles
Obsidian with Vim keybindings combines the best of both worlds: note-taking optimized UI with Vim's editing superpowers
Plain text as the foundation allows editing everything with muscle memory and sharing content across blog, second brain, and book with single scripts
money_quotes: That's how a surgeon would work: fine-grained and filigree, but with words.
I can edit my second brain, and text I write everywhere with the speed of thought.
It felt powerful. I had one version of the truth. No more formatting battles, no more fear I'd lose a bolding text or a code formatting.
It was like learning a game, adding a new superpower (shortcut) every week.
The problem was that I lost my productivity whenever I switched to another editor, program, or app.
Whenever I switched editors to a newer, better one, I had to re-learn all shortcuts and mappings.
key_insight: Adopting Vim motions and Markdown as universal tools creates a compound productivity advantage that transfers across all text editing contexts, from code to notes to long-form writing.
